mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals potential future monetary policy tightening by the Federal Reserve, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates strong consumer demand and a tight labor market, suggesting powerful economic momentum that drives corporate profits and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between RSAFS month-over-month growth and UNRATE level does not show a statistically significant positive relationship with SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of exceptionally low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS), the cost of capital is reduced, encouraging investment and risk-taking, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the correlation turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong consumer and business confidence in the economic outlook, leading to increased construction activity and broader economic growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods following a 3-month average HOUST growth exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic output, signaling reduced demand and negatively impacting corporate revenues and profits, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO 3-month annualized change falls below -2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a weakening labor market, reduced consumer spending power, and increased economic uncertainty, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and robust GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong overall economy with expanding aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate revenues and profits, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth over any 10-year rolling window.
